skill.name: kotlin-coroutines-expert
skill.description: Expert patterns for Kotlin Coroutines and Flow, covering structured concurrency, error handling, and testing.
skill.summary: Expert patterns for Kotlin Coroutines and Flow, covering structured concurrency, error handling, and...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 26 potential issues (24 external_commands, 2 weak cryptographic). Manual review confirms all are FALSE POSITIVES. The 'external_commands' detections are markdown backtick formatting (e.g., `coroutineScope`) not shell execution. The 'weak cryptographic' detections are misidentified content. This is a legitimate Kotlin Coroutines documentation skill with no security risks.

content.value_statement: Kotlin Coroutines and Flow can be complex to master. This skill provides expert patterns for structured concurrency, error handling, and testing asynchronous code.
actual_capabilities: Handle exceptions in coroutines using CoroutineExceptionHandler
Build reactive streams with Flow and StateFlow
Write unit tests for suspending functions using TestScope
Debug coroutine cancellation and memory leaks
limitations: Does not cover Android-specific coroutine APIs (ViewModelScope, lifecycleScope)
Does not include Spring WebFlux or backend-specific patterns
Does not provide runtime execution or code generation

use_cases.title: Backend Developer
use_cases.description: Implement async API calls and parallel data fetching in Kotlin microservices

use_cases.title: Android Developer
use_cases.description: Handle async UI updates and reactive data streams in Android apps
use_cases.target_user: Android developers working with modern Kotlin

use_cases.title: Code Reviewer
use_cases.description: Review coroutine code for proper error handling and structured concurrency patterns

prompt_templates.title: Basic Coroutine Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to set up a CoroutineScope for parallel API calls in Kotlin
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to fetch multiple data sources concurrently

prompt_templates.title: Flow Error Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I handle errors in a Kotlin Flow pipeline?
prompt_templates.scenario: When building reactive data streams with error resilience

prompt_templates.title: Coroutine Testing
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a unit test for a suspending function using TestScope
prompt_templates.scenario: When testing coroutine-based code with controlled timing

prompt_templates.title: Cancellation Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how to properly handle coroutine cancellation without catching CancellationException
prompt_templates.scenario: When debugging stuck coroutines or implementing cleanup logic

output_examples.input: How do I run two API calls in parallel and handle errors independently?
output_examples.output: Use supervisorScope to run async tasks where failure of one does not cancel the others. Example: val task1 = async { api.fetchA() }; val task2 = async { api.fetchB() }; Both can fail independently without affecting each other.

output_examples.input: When should I use StateFlow vs SharedFlow?
output_examples.output: Use StateFlow for UI state that needs to be retained and always has a current value. Use SharedFlow for one-time events like navigation or showing toast messages.

best_practices: Always use Dispatchers.IO for blocking I/O operations to avoid blocking the main thread
Cancel coroutine scopes when they are no longer needed (e.g., in ViewModel.onCleared)
Use TestScope and runTest for deterministic coroutine testing with virtual time
anti_patterns: Avoid GlobalScope - it breaks structured concurrency and can lead to memory leaks
Do not catch CancellationException unless you rethrow it - this breaks cooperative cancellation
Avoid using suspendCoroutine or suspendCancellableCoroutine when higher-level APIs suffice

faq.question: What is structured concurrency in Kotlin?
faq.answer: Structured concurrency ensures coroutines are tied to a lifecycle scope. When the scope cancels, all its child coroutines are cancelled, preventing leaks.

faq.question: When should I use supervisorScope vs coroutineScope?
faq.answer: Use coroutineScope when you want any failure to cancel all children. Use supervisorScope when you want children to fail independently without affecting siblings.

faq.question: How do I test coroutines?
faq.answer: Use TestScope and runTest from kotlinx.coroutines.test. Inject TestDispatcher to control virtual time for deterministic testing.

faq.question: What is the difference between Flow and StateFlow?
faq.answer: Flow is cold - it produces values only when collected. StateFlow is hot - it retains a current value and emits to all collectors.

faq.question: How do I handle exceptions in Flow?
faq.answer: Use catch operator at the end of the pipeline, or use try-catch inside the flow builder. For retrying, use retry or retryWhen operators.

faq.question: What are common causes of coroutine memory leaks?
faq.answer: Using GlobalScope, not cancelling scopes when done, capturing context incorrectly, or holding references to actors that are never completed.
